Inflation Signal
← Back to dashboard

Inflation Signal

Broad U.S. inflation signal from price-level dynamics.

As of 2026-04-30

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given the latest composite z-score of 0.004 and the sharp decline from 0.425, this setup aligns with Transition-biased price paths and Indeterminate volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not trend continuation.
  • The signal has entered a Transition state (per methodology thresholds), indicating a structural turning point or directionless period (1).
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Low Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes. Signal Stability Assessment: Volatile; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: High.

Methodology Applied:

  • Numerical thresholds: COOL is defined as a composite z-score ≤ -0.50, while TRANSITION is -0.25 to +0.25 (1).
  • Investment Implication: Transition regimes indicate high regime-flip risk; allocation should defer to component momentum (1).
  • Economic Meaning: A COOL regime or falling z-score implies a disinflationary impulse or demand deceleration (1).
  • Dataset: Inflation Signal (Latest observation: 2026-04-30).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the rapid compression of the composite z-score from 0.426 to 0.004 in a single period (1).
  • The signal exhibits high volatility, frequently oscillating between COOL and NEUTRAL states over the trailing twelve months (1).
  • Internal Tension: The provided data labels the current state as "COOL," but the methodology numerical thresholds classify it as "TRANSITION."
  • Conditional Invalidation: A composite z-score drop below -0.50 would confirm a COOL regime and disinflationary impulse (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant base case: Market remains in a directionless transition state pending clearer component momentum.
  • Most plausible upside risk: Shift to NEUTRAL or HOT if 3-month annualized CPI exceeds YoY CPI (1).
  • Most plausible downside risk: Definitive move into COOL regime (≤ -0.50) signaling demand deceleration (1).

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months); the signal is benchmarked against a 10-year rolling trend (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low; justified by the internal conflict between data labels and methodology thresholds.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the liquidity and pricing dimensions of the macro environment (1).
  • Economic mechanism: The downward trajectory implies weakening inflationary pressure or a potential disinflationary slowdown (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interacts with Fed Funds Rate and Broad USD Index to signal monetary policy shifts (1).

Regime Context:

  • The signal has newly entered the transition zone after a persistent but volatile neutral period.
  • Direction of change: Weakening (moving from neutral toward the disinflationary threshold).

Model Limitations:

  • Highly sensitive to CPI revisions, seasonality, and TIPS liquidity bias during market stress (1).

Data & References:

  • Inflation Signal composite series (2026-04-30).
  • Most influential: 0.004227 z-score (2026-04).
  • Recommended supplements: Headline CPI (3m Annualized) and 10Y Breakeven Inflation Rates.
